text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code readable and easy to understand for both yourself and others. A good starting point is to follow consistent naming conventions—variable and function names should clearly describe their purpose, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Another key practice is to break your code into small, focused functions, each responsible for a single task. This makes your program easier to test and debug. Ultimately, clean code improves maintainability, allowing you to modify or extend features in the future without confusion.
